use_case_name: Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS

structured.name: Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS
structured.objective:
To facilitate the management of the Charging Station client side certificate, a certificate update
procedure is provided.
structured.description:
The CSMS requests the Charging Station to update its key using TriggerMessageRequest with the
requestedMessage field set to SignChargingStationCertificate (or
SignV2GCertificate for separate ISO 15118 certificate).
If the Charging Station has a separate ISO15118Ctrlr (SECC in ISO 15118) for each EVSE, then
CSMS will have to send a request for each of them. The device model the Charging Station will tell
if ISO15118Ctrlr is located at toplevel or EVSE-level.
If the Charging Station has multiple SECCs that each control multiple EVSEs, then these are
represented in device model by an ISO15118Ctrlr for each EVSE. The EVSEs that are controlled by
the same SECC report an ISO15118Ctrlr with the same "SeccId".
scenario:
1. The CSMS requests the Charging Station to update its certificate using the
TriggerMessageRequest with the requestedMessage field set to SignChargingStationCertificate.
2. The Charging Station responds with TriggerMessageResponse
3. The Charging Station generates a new public / private key pair.

4. The Charging Station sends a SignCertificateRequest to the CSMS containing the
certificateType = ChargingStationCertificate .
5. The CSMS responds with SignCertificateResponse, with status Accepted.
6. The CSMS forwards the CSR to the Certificate Authority Server.
7. Certificate Authority Server signs the certificate.
8. The Certificate Authority Server returns the Signed Certificate to the CSMS.
9. The CSMS sends CertificateSignedRequest to the Charging Station.
0. The Charging Station verifies the Signed Certificate.

1. The Charging Station responds with CertificateSignedResponse to the CSMS with the status
Accepted or Rejected.

requirements.definition:
A key update SHOULD be performed after
installation of the Charging Station, to change the
key from the one initially provisioned by the
manufacturer (possibly a default key).

The Charging Station SHALL generate a new public
/ private key pair using one of the key generation
functions described in Section 4.2.1.3 of [16].

The Charging Station SHALL send the public key in
form of a Certificate Signing Request (CSR) as
described in RFC 2986 [22] and then PEM encoded,
using the SignCertificateRequest message.

The CSMS SHOULD NOT sign the certificate itself,
but instead forwards the CSR to a dedicated
certificate authority server managing the
certificates for the Charging Station infrastructure.
The dedicated authority server MAY be operated by
the CSO.

The private key generated by the Charging Station
during the key update process SHALL NOT leave
the Charging Station at any time, and SHALL NOT
be readable via OCPP or any other (remote)
communication connection.

The Charging Station SHALL verify the validity of
the signed certificate in the
CertificateSignedRequest message, checking that
the current date (at the time of the update) is within
the certificate’s validity period, the properties in
Certificate Properties, and that it is part of the
Charging Station Operator certificate hierarchy as
described in Certificate Hierarchy.

requirements.precondition:
If the certificate is not
valid.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL respond to the
CertificateSignedRequest with status Rejected AND
discard the certificate AND trigger an
InvalidChargingStationCertificate security event
(See part 2 appendices for the full list of security
events).

The Charging Station SHALL respond to the
CertificateSignedRequest with status Accepted
AND
the Charging Station SHALL switch to the new
certificate by reconnecting the websocket and TLS
connection.

requirements.precondition:
(A02.FR.08 OR
A02.FR.28) AND
The Charging Station
successfully connected
to the CSMS using either
one of the certificates.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL discard the client
certificate that is NOT in use.

requirements.precondition:
Upon receipt of a
SignCertificateRequest
AND
It is able to process the
request
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL set status to Accepted in the
SignCertificateResponse.

requirements.precondition:
Upon receipt of a
SignCertificateRequest
AND
It is NOT able to process
the request
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL set status to Rejected in the
SignCertificateResponse.

requirements.precondition:
When using different
certificates for 15118
connections and the
Charging Station to
CSMS connection
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL set the certificateType
field in the SignCertificateRequest to the certificate
for which the update was triggered.

requirements.precondition:
When receiving a
SignCertificateRequest
with certificateType
included
requirements.definition:
It is RECOMMENDED for the CSMS to set the
certificateType field in the CertificateSignedRequest
to the type of certificate in the
SignCertificateRequest.

requirements.precondition:
If the Charging Station
contains more than one
valid V2G certificate,
derived from the same
root certificate.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL use the newest
certificate, as measured by the start of the validity
period.

requirements.precondition:
If the configuration
variable
MaxCertificateChainSize
is implemented AND
The Charging Station
receives a
CertificateSignedRequest
message with a
certificate (chain) with a
size that exceeds the set
value configured at
MaxCertificateChainSize
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY respond with a
CertificateSignedResponse message with status
Rejected .

requirements.precondition:
When the CSMS
accepted the
SignCertificateRequest
for a CSR AND
the Charging Station did
not yet receive a
CertificateSignedRequest
for this CSR AND
the number of seconds
configured at
CertSigningWaitMinimu
m has expired
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a new
SignCertificateRequest for the CSR. Optionally, this
CSR MAY be for a newly generated key pair.

The Charging Station SHALL double the previous
back-off time, starting with the number of seconds
configured at CertSigningWaitMinimum, every time
the back-off time expires without having received
the CertificateSignedRequest for this CSR.

requirements.precondition:
A02.FR.18 AND
The maximum number of
increments is reached
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop resending the
SignCertificateRequest, until it is requested by the
CSMS via a TriggerMessageRequest for
SignChargingStationCertificate,
SignV2GCertificate or
SignCombinedCertificate.

The Charging Station SHALL NOT initiate the back-
off mechanism and resend the
SignCertificateRequest, until this is requested by
the CSMS via a TriggerMessageRequest for
SignChargingStationCertificate,
SignV2GCertificate or
SignCombinedCertificate.

requirements.precondition:
When the Charging
Station receives a
SignCertificateResponse
with status Rejected, in
response to a
SignCertificateRequest
with certificateType
V2GCertificate
requirements.definition:
It is RECOMMENDED to turn off
ISO15118PnCEnabled until the Charging Station
has been rebooted.

requirements.precondition:
A02.FR.08 AND
the charging station was
not able to successfully
connect to any of the
configured entries of
NetworkConfigurati
onPriority using the
new certificate AND
The Charging Station
supports either one or
both reconnection
mechanisms described
at requirements;
B10.FR.07 and
B10.FR.08.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL for the reconnection
mechanism described at B10.FR.07 fallback to the
old client certificate AND
for the reconnection mechanism described at
B10.FR.08 alternate between using the old and
new client certificate after all
NetworkConfigurationPriority entries.

requirements.precondition:
A02.FR.10 AND
The Charging Station
discarded the new client
certificate.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHOULD send a
SecurityEventNotification
DiscardedRenewedClientCertificate to the CSMS.

chunks.content:
# A02 - Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS

**Objective:** To facilitate the management of the Charging Station client side certificate, a certificate update
procedure is provided.

**Description:** The CSMS requests the Charging Station to update its key using TriggerMessageRequest with the
requestedMessage field set to SignChargingStationCertificate (or
SignV2GCertificate for separate ISO 15118 certificate).
If the Charging Station has a separate ISO15118Ctrlr (SECC in ISO 15118) for each EVSE, then
CSMS will have to send a request for each of them. The device model the Charging Station will tell
if ISO15118Ctrlr is located at toplevel or EVSE-level.
If the Charging Station has multiple SECCs that each control multiple EVSEs, then these are
represented in device model by an ISO15118Ctrlr for each EVSE. The EVSEs that are controlled by
the same SECC report an ISO15118Ctrlr with the same "SeccId".

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS, Certificate Authority Server

**Scenario:**
SignChargingStationCertificate
1. The CSMS requests the Charging Station to update its certificate using the
TriggerMessageRequest with the requestedMessage field set to SignChargingStationCertificate.
2. The Charging Station responds with TriggerMessageResponse
3. The Charging Station generates a new public / private key pair.
4. The Charging Station sends a SignCertificateRequest to the CSMS containing the
certificateType = ChargingStationCertificate .
5. The CSMS responds with SignCertificateResponse, with status Accepted.
6. The CSMS forwards the CSR to the Certificate Authority Server.
7. Certificate Authority Server signs the certificate.
8. The Certificate Authority Server returns the Signed Certificate to the CSMS.
9. The CSMS sends CertificateSignedRequest to the Charging Station.
0. The Charging Station verifies the Signed Certificate.
1. The Charging Station responds with CertificateSignedResponse to the CSMS with the status
Accepted or Rejected.

metadata.heading: A02 - Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS
metadata.use_case_name: Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS

chunks.content:
# A02 - Requirements

**A02.FR.01**
A key update SHOULD be performed after
installation of the Charging Station, to change the
key from the one initially provisioned by the
manufacturer (possibly a default key).

**A02.FR.02**
Precondition: After sending a
TriggerMessageRespons
e.
The Charging Station SHALL generate a new public
/ private key pair using one of the key generation
functions described in Section 4.2.1.3 of [16].

**A02.FR.03**
Precondition: A02.FR.02
The Charging Station SHALL send the public key in
form of a Certificate Signing Request (CSR) as
described in RFC 2986 [22] and then PEM encoded,
using the SignCertificateRequest message.

**A02.FR.04**
The CSMS SHOULD NOT sign the certificate itself,
but instead forwards the CSR to a dedicated
certificate authority server managing the
certificates for the Charging Station infrastructure.
The dedicated authority server MAY be operated by
the CSO.

**A02.FR.05**
The private key generated by the Charging Station
during the key update process SHALL NOT leave
the Charging Station at any time, and SHALL NOT
be readable via OCPP or any other (remote)
communication connection.

**A02.FR.06**
The Charging Station SHALL verify the validity of
the signed certificate in the
CertificateSignedRequest message, checking that
the current date (at the time of the update) is within
the certificate’s validity period, the properties in
Certificate Properties, and that it is part of the
Charging Station Operator certificate hierarchy as
described in Certificate Hierarchy.

**A02.FR.07**
Precondition: If the certificate is not
valid.
The Charging Station SHALL respond to the
CertificateSignedRequest with status Rejected AND
discard the certificate AND trigger an
InvalidChargingStationCertificate security event
(See part 2 appendices for the full list of security
events).

**A02.FR.08**
Precondition: If the certificate is valid.
The Charging Station SHALL respond to the
CertificateSignedRequest with status Accepted
AND
the Charging Station SHALL switch to the new
certificate by reconnecting the websocket and TLS
connection.

**A02.FR.10**
Precondition: (A02.FR.08 OR
A02.FR.28) AND
The Charging Station
successfully connected
to the CSMS using either
one of the certificates.
The Charging Station SHALL discard the client
certificate that is NOT in use.

metadata.heading: A02 - Requirements
metadata.use_case_name: Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS

chunks.content:
# A02 - Requirements (continued)

**A02.FR.11**
Precondition: Upon receipt of a
SignCertificateRequest
AND
It is able to process the
request
The CSMS SHALL set status to Accepted in the
SignCertificateResponse.

**A02.FR.12**
Precondition: Upon receipt of a
SignCertificateRequest
AND
It is NOT able to process
the request
The CSMS SHALL set status to Rejected in the
SignCertificateResponse.

**A02.FR.13**
Precondition: When using different
certificates for 15118
connections and the
Charging Station to
CSMS connection
The Charging Station SHALL set the certificateType
field in the SignCertificateRequest to the certificate
for which the update was triggered.

**A02.FR.14**
Precondition: When receiving a
SignCertificateRequest
with certificateType
included
It is RECOMMENDED for the CSMS to set the
certificateType field in the CertificateSignedRequest
to the type of certificate in the
SignCertificateRequest.

**A02.FR.15**
Precondition: If the Charging Station
contains more than one
valid V2G certificate,
derived from the same
root certificate.
The Charging Station SHALL use the newest
certificate, as measured by the start of the validity
period.

**A02.FR.16**
Precondition: If the configuration
variable
MaxCertificateChainSize
is implemented AND
The Charging Station
receives a
CertificateSignedRequest
message with a
certificate (chain) with a
size that exceeds the set
value configured at
MaxCertificateChainSize
The Charging Station MAY respond with a
CertificateSignedResponse message with status
Rejected .

**A02.FR.17**
Precondition: When the CSMS
accepted the
SignCertificateRequest
for a CSR AND
the Charging Station did
not yet receive a
CertificateSignedRequest
for this CSR AND
the number of seconds
configured at
CertSigningWaitMinimu
m has expired
The Charging Station SHALL send a new
SignCertificateRequest for the CSR. Optionally, this
CSR MAY be for a newly generated key pair.

**A02.FR.18**
Precondition: A02.FR.17
The Charging Station SHALL double the previous
back-off time, starting with the number of seconds
configured at CertSigningWaitMinimum, every time
the back-off time expires without having received
the CertificateSignedRequest for this CSR.

**A02.FR.19**
Precondition: A02.FR.18 AND
The maximum number of
increments is reached
The Charging Station SHALL stop resending the
SignCertificateRequest, until it is requested by the
CSMS via a TriggerMessageRequest for
SignChargingStationCertificate,
SignV2GCertificate or
SignCombinedCertificate.

metadata.heading: A02 - Requirements
metadata.use_case_name: Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS

chunks.content:
# A02 - Requirements (continued)

**A02.FR.20**
Precondition: A02.FR.07
The Charging Station SHALL NOT initiate the back-
off mechanism and resend the
SignCertificateRequest, until this is requested by
the CSMS via a TriggerMessageRequest for
SignChargingStationCertificate,
SignV2GCertificate or
SignCombinedCertificate.

**A02.FR.21**
Precondition: When the Charging
Station receives a
SignCertificateResponse
with status Rejected, in
response to a
SignCertificateRequest
with certificateType
V2GCertificate
It is RECOMMENDED to turn off
ISO15118PnCEnabled until the Charging Station
has been rebooted.

**A02.FR.28**
Precondition: A02.FR.08 AND
the charging station was
not able to successfully
connect to any of the
configured entries of
NetworkConfigurati
onPriority using the
new certificate AND
The Charging Station
supports either one or
both reconnection
mechanisms described
at requirements;
B10.FR.07 and
B10.FR.08.
The Charging Station SHALL for the reconnection
mechanism described at B10.FR.07 fallback to the
old client certificate AND
for the reconnection mechanism described at
B10.FR.08 alternate between using the old and
new client certificate after all
NetworkConfigurationPriority entries.

**A02.FR.29**
Precondition: A02.FR.10 AND
The Charging Station
discarded the new client
certificate.
The Charging Station SHOULD send a
SecurityEventNotification
DiscardedRenewedClientCertificate to the CSMS.
